Each proposal should stem from a simple and elegant question, observation, or hypothesis about the topic. {"idea": {"Name": "spectral_stability_routing", "Title": "Spectral Stability Routing: Training-Free Mixed-Precision Dispatch via Activation Covariance Geometry", "Short Hypothesis": "Local activation covariance condition numbers and spectral decay rates serve as zero-cost, training-free proxies for numerical stability radii. Investigating this in dynamic precision routing is optimal because it isolates the direct causal link between representation geometry and hardware-level numerical stability, bypassing the confounding variables of gradient-based calibration or heuristic error thresholds. No simpler method exists to guarantee precision-switching safety without empirical fine-tuning or static worst-case bounds.", "Related Work": "Existing dynamic precision methods rely on calibration data, gradient approximations, or learned routers, introducing training overhead or heuristic fragility.
